helpful: Provisioned questions-presented.txt, petition.txt, and brief-in-opposition.txt were decisive — the BIO's finality and alternative-holding arguments drove the probability more than any base rate.
statpack relist-count and CVSG cuts gave usable quantitative anchors.
gaps: fedcourts query returned zero rows for citation filters on the Miller/Montgomery/Jones line (both calls); citation-linked retrieval seems not to cover modern cert petitions, so no comparable resolved priors could be pulled.
The committed statpack.md lacks the per-Term salience-band table the prompt says to anchor on; fee-class (paid vs IFP) grant rates are also only in statpack.json, not the committed markdown.
A signal cut for petitioner identity (state/US/private, SG-represented) would sharpen exactly the adjustment this case turns on.
notes: Forward cell; snapshot one day old and CourtListener had no record of the docket, so provisioned inputs were effectively the retrieval ceiling in practice.
